title: Lippia schlechtendalii
taxonomy: Lippia schlechtendalii is a species of flowering plant in the verbena family, Verbenaceae. It is native to South America, specifically in the tropical regions of Brazil and Argentina. Lippia schlechtendalii belongs to the genus Lippia, which is a group of flowering plants commonly known as the lippia or verbena. The genus Lippia is further classified under the family Verbenaceae, which is a family of flowering plants that includes about 250 species. This plant is also known to be a member of the order Lamiales, a large and diverse order of flowering plants.

intro: Lippia schlechtendalii, also known as Mexican citronella or sweet verbena, is a tropical plant native to Central and South America, belonging to the family Verbenaceae. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including the reduction of inflammation, antimicrobial activity, and the alleviation of symptoms associated with fever and colds. The bioactive constituents of Lippia schlechtendalii include essential oils, flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenes, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Lippia schlechtendalii include teas, infusions, and essential oil extracts, which are often used to relieve digestive issues, promote relaxation, and support immune function.
